results.title: Privacy Policy | Pluno AI
results.content:
If you have questions, requests, or complaints about privacy at Pluno, contact us atprivacy@pluno.ai.

You may also have the right to contact your local data protection authority or privacy regulator.

results.title: About | Pluno AI
results.content:
## How did it all start?

Pluno was born out of a desire to solve real, impactful problems for growing businesses. In 2021, Alex and Korbi, two AI engineers with experience in B2B SaaS startups, made the bold decision to leave their roles and focus entirely on tackling challenges that demanded smarter solutions.

During their journey, they discovered a recurring issue faced by many teams: managing customer inquiries at scale was inefficient, time-consuming, and often frustrating. Determined to address this, they developed a simple prototype, known at the time as AwesomeQA. [...] Skip to content

# Why we built Pluno

Pluno was born from a simple idea: make customer support smarter, more efficient, and less frustrating for teams managing inquiries at scale. What started as a prototype called AwesomeQA - created to tackle repetitive questions - has evolved into a powerful platform transforming how modern SaaS businesses handle support.

Today, Pluno does more than just deflect routine inquiries. It streamlines escalation processes, automates workflows, and fosters seamless collaboration between teams, all while ensuring exceptional customer interactions. From startups to enterprises, we empower businesses worldwide to work smarter, resolve issues faster, and keep customers happier. [...] The feedback from early users became the foundation of something much bigger. AwesomeQA has since evolved into what is now known as Pluno—a platform designed not only to deflect repetitive inquiries but also to streamline the entire escalation support process for modern SaaS businesses. By automating workflows, enabling seamless customer interactions, and supporting collaboration, Pluno empowers teams to work smarter and serve customers better.

From humble beginnings to helping businesses worldwide, our mission remains the same: to create tools that make your work easier and your customers happier.

Pluno founders Alex and Korbi

We push hard and have fun along the way.

Team gathering

Team cycling

Team race

Team outing

Team scenery

## Supported by the best:

North Island Ventures

results.title: Changelog | Pluno AI
results.content:
Special thanks to our users SolaX Power, Innovorder, Kojo, Smartness and BudgetBakers for their feedback that inspired these updates.

## Meet the new Deflection Agent in Zendesk

Meet the new Deflection Agent in Zendesk

Other deflection agents may have failed you with generic answers, frustrating loops, or even hallucinations. Now, experience a deflection agent fine-tuned to your domain, trained on your space’s context, a full-fledged, advanced knowledge-bearing support agent, just like an in-house trained expert. Deflects queries directly within Zendesk (supports both messaging and tickets), and when it can’t, escalates seamlessly to your team while gathering all the necessary information. Your team also gets full access to Pluno’s dashboard for effortless knowledge base management. [...] Resolved an issue where Zendesk web chats were not correctly associated with users who had multiple emails linked to an existing account.
 You can now disable Pluno for ticket deflection completely. Previously, once an agent was selected for Pluno to answer as, it always handled their tickets even without an active trigger.
 When using the web widget, end users no longer have to provide their email for escalation if they are authenticated.
 Fixed an issue where the Pluno AI agent continued responding after escalation to a team member.
 Fixed an issue where the channel selector for linking to an existing Jira ticket showed all Jira issues, causing child issues to be mistakenly selected as parents. The drop-down now only displays parent issues. [...] Fixed an issue where Pluno did not account for unreadable attachments; prompts now mention that an attachment exists even when its contents cannot be accessed.

results.title: Troubleshooting Agent | Pluno AI
results.content:
Both, and it knows the difference. If the root cause is a bug, Pluno escalates to your engineering Slack channel with a full report and a recommended fix. If the cause is customer-side (configuration, misuse, data formatting), Pluno posts the resolution as an internal note on the ticket so support can close it without involving engineering.

Only when you let it. Pluno proposes a fix and asks for approval before opening a PR. You can also configure it to auto-PR for specific repos or specific kinds of issues, with a human review gate before merge. [...] 02

### Customers waiting in line

Support waits on engineering. Customers wait on support. SLAs slip, CSAT drops.

03

### Incidents found by your queue

Regressions surface ten tickets in. Queue's on fire, on-call's up at 2am.

This is the loop Pluno breaks.

## 3 ways Pluno Troubleshooting Agent saves engineers from unnecessary work

### Finds bugs and recommends fixes,all by itself

How it flows

1. 1Support ticket comes in
2. 2Investigates codebase, DB, Sentry, session recordings, logs
3. 3Reports findings on Slack #engineering with a recommended fix

Slack · #engineering

Pluno

Pluno· just now

Checkout failure ticket. Latest billing deploy regressed for EU accounts missing `billing_country`. Fix attached. Open a PR? [...] 5×

Faster Resolutions

### Support Agents Empowered

When it's not a bug, Pluno drafts the customer-facing fix inside the ticket. Support resolves on first response.

24/7

incident detection

### Catch incidents early

Pluno spots regressions hitting multiple customers and alerts before the queue floods.

Built for

## The people who get pinged when things break

Whichever team owns the ticket, Pluno does the first investigation. Humans only step in when humans need to.

 Support engineers

  First-line investigation
 Escalation teams

  Triaging to engineering
 Solutions engineers

  Customer-specific debugging
 Product engineers

  Pulled in for hard tickets
 Incident responders

  Customer-impacting issues

3,000+ integrations
